steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the object of "prevent ___ from joining the ranks of the extinct." It refers back to "many even-toed ungulates" — a plural noun — so the pronoun must be plural and function as a direct object (not possessive).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the animal: Singular. "Many even-toed ungulates" is plural; using a singular noun misrepresents the antecedent. ✗
  • Choice B — these animals: Plural and non-possessive. Correctly refers back to "many even-toed ungulates" and fills the object slot — "prevent these animals from joining." ✓
  • Choice C — this animal's: Singular and possessive. Both flaws apply: it mismatches the plural antecedent and a possessive cannot serve as the direct object of "prevent." ✗
  • Choice D — these animals': Plural but possessive. A possessive cannot be a direct object — "prevent these animals' from joining" is ungrammatical. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
